title: CCT benchmark compare-config
description: Configuration consumed by `./scripts/benchmark compare --config <path>`. Lists multiple (backend, model, env) candidates to evaluate against the same benchmark task set; the harness runs them sequentially under one parent run-dir and aggregates the report.

benchmark.description: Benchmark adapter id, as listed by `./scripts/benchmark list` (e.g. 'aider-polyglot', 'stub', 'cct-dogfood-memkernel').

runs.description: Number of repetitions per task per candidate. Higher values reduce stdev and enable the calibrated winner-declaration rule; 3 is the recommended floor for cross-LLM comparisons.

task.description: Optional task-id filter; same shape as `./scripts/benchmark run --task <id>` (repeatable). Omit to run every task the adapter exposes.

attempt_timeout_seconds.description: Optional per-attempt wall-clock timeout (seconds). NOT YET WIRED AT RUNTIME: as of D3 this key is schema-accepted but the compare config parser (`compare._validate`/`CompareConfig`) does not read or retain it, so a config that sets it currently runs with the harness's existing default timeout behaviour. D5 adds the parser field and threads it to the backend via RunContext.timeout_seconds, at which point a timed-out attempt is recorded result='timeout', counted as a pass_rate failure, and flagged separately in verdicts, and the wrapper's cloud/local heuristic default (scripts/bench: 300s cloud, 600s when ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL is non-default) applies when this key is omitted. See specs/benchmark-bench-driver/spec.md § D5.

candidates.description: Two or more LLM candidates to compare. Single-candidate comparisons are rejected — use `./scripts/benchmark run` for that. Each candidate runs sequentially in declaration order.

name.description: Human-readable label for this candidate; appears in compare-manifest.json. Defaults to '<backend>:<model>'. Must be unique across all candidates; set explicitly when (backend, model) overlaps but env routing differs.

backend.description: Backend family (copilot CLI), e.g. 'claude-code', 'stub'. The deprecated combined form 'claude-code:sonnet' is rejected — split into separate backend + model fields.

model.description: Model identifier passed to the backend, e.g. 'sonnet', 'opus', 'claude-sonnet-4-6', or — when routing through a gateway — the model name the gateway serves (e.g. 'meta-llama/Llama-3-70B-Instruct'). The stub backend ignores this field.

env.description: Provider-routing env vars applied only for this candidate's runs and restored afterward. Typical keys: ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL (for Claude Code's gateway support — see https://code.claude.com/docs/en/llm-gateway). VALUES MAY CONTAIN SECRETS and are read into os.environ for the candidate's runs; only the KEY NAMES are persisted to compare-manifest.json.
